task:
# Relay stage 5: Author-tests
Task: 07-centralize-colors-and-font-sizes
Working directory: /Users/admin/Dev/temp-visual-relay/

## Task input
# Centralize Colors and Font Sizes, Then Make Them More Accessible

## Problem / motivation

Colors and font sizes are hard-coded literally throughout the UI, so there is no way to audit — or
improve — the palette or the type scale without reading every view. Today the tree carries **~235 raw
hex literals** across `src/**/*.axaml` and **~107 inline `FontSize="…"`** attributes spread over 15
views, plus a handful of programmatic sites in C#. A color like `#2A303A` recurs in several card borders
with no name telling you it is "the same" border; the font scale is an undocumented spread of
`10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 16`, including sizes small enough to be a legibility problem.

This one task does three inseparable things:

1. Centralize every color and font size into named **design tokens** (Avalonia resources) and make
   **all** code reference those tokens.
2. Add a guard test that **fails if any raw color or font-size literal reappears**.
3. Once everything resolves to a token, **tidy the token values to be more accessible** — fix low
   contrast and raise too-small text to meet WCAG. This is the real payoff: the centralization exists so
   this cleanup is possible in one small, reviewable place.

There is **no ratchet and no baseline of grandfathered violations** — add the guard first, let it
enumerate every offending file, migrate them all, then adjust the token values, and land it with every
gate green.

## The single source of truth

Avalonia already separates `Application.Resources` (a `ResourceDictionary`) from `Application.Styles`
(a `Styles` collection). Tokens are resources; put them there. `App.axaml`
(`src/VisualRelay.App/App.axaml`) currently declares only `<Application.Styles>` (a `FluentTheme` plus
`StyleInclude` of `VisualRelayTheme.axaml`) and has **no** `<Application.Resources>` yet — add it.

Create the tokens in **new** files (the ≤300-line file-size guard forbids stuffing them into the already
large `VisualRelayTheme.axaml`):

- `src/VisualRelay.App/Theme/Colors.axaml` — a `ResourceDictionary` defining a **semantic** palette. For
  each distinct color in use, define a `Color` and a matching `SolidColorBrush` (e.g. `SurfaceBrush`,
  `SurfaceRaisedBrush`, `BorderSubtleBrush`, `TextPrimaryBrush`, `TextMutedBrush`, `AccentBrush`,
  `AccentHoverBrush`, `WarningBrush`, `WarningTextBrush`, `DropIndicatorBrush`, …). **Collapse exact
  duplicates** — the several borders that are all `#2A303A` become one `BorderSubtleBrush`; that
  de-duplication is the audit win.
- `src/VisualRelay.App/Theme/Typography.axaml` — a `ResourceDictionary` of `x:Double` font-size tokens,
  **one per distinct value currently in use** (`10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 16`), named by role (e.g.
  `FontSizeCaption`, `FontSizeBody`, `FontSizeTitle` — you choose the mapping). Do **not** invent new
  sizes at this step; every existing `FontSize` must map onto one of these (values are adjusted later,
  under accessibility).

Merge both into `App.axaml` via `<Application.Resources><ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>` with a
`<ResourceInclude Source="avares://VisualRelay.App/Theme/Colors.axaml"/>` (and Typography). The app uses
a single fixed `RequestedThemeVariant="Dark"` — keep it to **one** palette; do **not** add light/dark
`ThemeDictionaries` the app does not use.

These two token files are the **only** place raw color/size literals may live. Everything else references
them.

## Migrate every usage to tokens (value-preserving)

Replace literals with resource references everywhere, carrying the **exact current values** so this step
changes nothing visually:

- **Style setters** in `src/VisualRelay.App/Styles/VisualRelayTheme.axaml` — e.g. the `Selector="Button"`
  setter `<Setter Property="Background" Value="#1A1E25"/>` becomes
  `Value="{DynamicResource SurfaceRaisedBrush}"`; `Button.primary`'s `#5B7CFA`, the `Button.warning`
  swatch, the `ListBoxItem.drop-above`/`drop-below` `#3191FF`, the `Border.queueCard` colors, etc.
- **Inline attributes** in every view under `src/VisualRelay.App/Views/**` — `Foreground="…"`,
  `Background="…"`, `BorderBrush="…"`, and `FontSize="…"` become `{DynamicResource …}` (brushes) /
  `{StaticResource …}` (font sizes). Use `DynamicResource` for brushes, `StaticResource` for the
  `x:Double` font sizes (which never change at runtime); the guard accepts either markup extension.
- **The `#`-color inside `BoxShadow` and any `GradientStop`** — the color component must reference a
  token; keep offsets/blur numeric.
- **Programmatic sites in C#** (few, but they must not become an escape hatch):
  - `src/VisualRelay.App/Views/Controls/ChevronIcon.cs` — the `Foreground` `StyledProperty` default
    `new SolidColorBrush(Color.Parse("#6F7785"))`, and the `Foreground ?? Brushes.Gray` fallback in its
    render path.
  - `src/VisualRelay.App/Views/Controls/FocusToggleIcon.cs` — the same `Foreground ?? Brushes.Gray`
    fallback.
  - `src/VisualRelay.App/ViewModels/TaskRowViewModel.cs` — `RailBrush`/`RunningBrush`/`SelectedBrush`
    and `Brushes.Transparent`.
  - `src/VisualRelay.App/Views/Controls/Buttons/CommonButton.cs` — `FontSize = 14`.
  - `src/VisualRelay.App/App.axaml.cs` — the confirmation-dialog factory's `FontSize = 13`.

  Prefer moving control-default brushes/sizes **out of C# into theme `Style` setters** that use the
  tokens (a `StyledProperty` default evaluates at type-init, before app resources exist, so it cannot
  take a `DynamicResource`). For genuinely code-built UI, resolve the token from resources
  (`Application.Current!.FindResource("…")`). Note: `ChevronIcon`'s foreground default is asserted
  non-null by an existing test, `ChevronForeground_HasExplicitDefault_NotNull` in
  `tests/VisualRelay.Tests/ChevronAffordanceRenderTests.cs`; if you relocate that default to a Style,
  update that assertion to check the styled/token value rather than deleting or weakening it.

Only two literal color values are exempt from tokenizing (they are not palette colors): `Transparent`
and `{x:Null}`. Everything else resolves to a token.

## The guard: no raw literals may return

Add `tests/VisualRelay.Tests/DesignTokenCentralizationTests.cs`, modeled structurally on the existing
`ButtonsCentralizationTests` (`tests/VisualRelay.Tests/ButtonsCentralizationTests.cs`): discover the
repo via `RepoSetup.Root`, enumerate source files, collect every offender as
`relativePath:line → text`, and assert the violation list is empty with a message that **lists every
offender** so the failure is the worklist. Allow-list the two token files (`Theme/Colors.axaml`,
`Theme/Typography.axaml`) as the sole place literals may appear.

Follow the `FileSizeGuard` seam (`tools/VisualRelay.Guards`, unit-tested by `FileSizeGuardTests`): put
the classification in a **pure** function (e.g. `DesignTokenGuard.FindViolations(files)`) covered by
in-memory unit tests, and a separate real-tree `[Fact]` that runs it over `src/VisualRelay.App` and
asserts zero.

Rules the guard enforces:

1. **No `#`-hex color literal** (`#RGB`/`#RRGGBB`/`#AARRGGBB`) anywhere in `.axaml` outside the two
   token files. A hex literal has one unambiguous lexical form, so a scan is complete for this case and
   catches inline attributes, `Setter` values, `BoxShadow`, and gradient stops alike.
2. **`FontSize` must be a resource reference.** For a `FontSize="…"` attribute or a
   `<Setter Property="FontSize" Value="…"/>`, the value must be `{StaticResource …}` or
   `{DynamicResource …}`; a numeric literal (`FontSize="12"`) fails. Scope this rule to `FontSize` only —
   raw numbers on `Width`, `Padding`, `Opacity`, `CornerRadius`, `BorderThickness`, etc. are out of scope
   (this task is colors and font sizes, nothing else).
3. **Named-color keywords** (`Red`, `White`, `Gray`, …) in the brush-bearing attributes (`Foreground`,
   `Background`, `BorderBrush`, `Fill`, `Stroke`) must be a resource reference, except the two exempt
   literals `Transparent` and `{x:Null}`.
4. **C# scan** over `src/VisualRelay.App/**/*.cs` (model on `ButtonsCentralizationTests.FindNewButtonExpressions`):
   flag `Color.Parse("#…")`, `Color.FromRgb`/`FromArgb`, `Colors.<Name>`, `Brushes.<Name>`,
   `new SolidColorBrush(`, and `FontSize = <numeric-literal>`. Allow-list a single constants file if one
   is introduced for type-init-safe defaults.

Rules 1 and 3 are the allow-list model we want (a styling value must resolve to a token); rule 1 uses a
scan only because hex has a single form, so scanning is already exhaustive for it.

**Add the guard first.** Its first run over the current tree enumerates every file to change — that list
*is* the migration worklist. Then migrate until the guard is green. Because there is no baseline, the
guard must be **red on the pre-migration tree and green after**.

## Tidy the centralized values to be more accessible

With every color and size resolving to one token, fix the accessibility problems the scattered literals
were hiding. The migration above is value-preserving; the adjustments here are the **only** intended
visual changes, and each must be justified against a WCAG 2.2 AA target.

- **Contrast.** Audit each foreground/background token pairing against WCAG 2.2 AA: **4.5:1** for normal
  text, **3:1** for large text (≥ 18px, or ≥ 14px bold) and for non-text UI (structural borders, the
  drop/focus indicators, state-carrying icons). Where a pair fails, nudge the token's lightness to meet
  the threshold while preserving its hue/identity. The likely offenders in the current palette are the
  **muted text**, the **warning** swatch (`#F0CA66` on `#2B2416`), and the **accent/link** colors
  (`#5B7CFA` / `#5575F2`) — verify all of them, not just these.
- **Type size.** Raise the floor of the scale: no text token below **12px**. Remap the former `10` and
  `11` up to at least `12` (the `11` is the most common size, so this is a deliberate, visible legibility
  change — verify no layout or density breaks). Keep the scale coherent; do not add sizes beyond the
  existing tokens.
- **Lock it in.** Add `tests/VisualRelay.Tests/DesignTokenAccessibilityTests.cs`: a **pure** WCAG
  relative-luminance / contrast-ratio function (unit-tested against known pairs — e.g. black-on-white =
  21:1, identical colors = 1:1), plus a `[Fact]` that resolves the token colors (parse
  `Theme/Colors.axaml`) and asserts every declared foreground/background pairing meets its AA threshold,
  and that no font-size token in `Theme/Typography.axaml` is below 12. Encode the real pairings as an
  explicit table in the test — the design's actual text-on-surface combinations — so any future token
  edit that breaks contrast fails here. Same pure-function-plus-real-data shape as
  `FileSizeGuard`/`FileSizeGuardTests`.

## Precedent to model on

- `tests/VisualRelay.Tests/ButtonsCentralizationTests.cs` — same problem family (a UI centralization
  rule enforced by scanning `.axaml` + `.cs`, allow-listing the central home, emitting a numbered
  offender list, asserting zero). Copy its shape.
- `tools/VisualRelay.Guards/FileSizeGuard` + `tests/VisualRelay.Tests/FileSizeGuardTests.cs` — the
  pure-`FindViolations` + real-tree-`Enumerate` split, exercised with in-memory/temp inputs.
- `RepoSetup.Root` — the repo-root discovery helper both guards use.

## Constraints & done criteria

- **The centralization guard is red on the pre-migration tree and green after** (`Failed: 0`). No
  baseline, no suppression list, no grandfathered offenders.
- **The pure classifier is unit-tested** in-memory: a hex line fails; `{DynamicResource SurfaceBrush}`
  passes; `Transparent` and `{x:Null}` pass; `FontSize="12"` fails while `FontSize="{StaticResource
  FontSizeBody}"` passes; a raw number on a non-styling attribute (`Width="12"`) is ignored.
- **Centralization is value-preserving; the accessibility pass is the only intended visual change.**
  After migrating, the app renders identically; then the deliberate accessibility adjustments (contrast
  to AA, 12px type floor) are the only pixels that move, each justified by a WCAG target. Verify the
  running app.
- **Every distinct existing font size (`10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 16`) maps to exactly one token** and no
  `FontSize` literal remains; after the accessibility pass **no font-size token is below 12px**.
- **Every declared foreground/background pairing meets WCAG 2.2 AA** (4.5:1 text / 3:1 large-text & UI),
  enforced by `DesignTokenAccessibilityTests`; its contrast function is unit-tested (black-on-white =
  21:1).
- **Never weaken, skip, or delete a test** to satisfy any gate (VR's own guards forbid `Skip`/deletion);
  update the Chevron default-foreground assertion as described rather than removing it.
- **Keep every new/edited `*.cs`/`*.axaml` file ≤ 300 lines** — hence tokens in separate `Theme/*.axaml`
  files, and split a test file if it grows past the limit.
- Do not tokenize or alter non-color/size literals (margins, padding, radii, thicknesses, durations) —
  out of scope.

## Files likely in scope (the plan stage will finalize the manifest)

- `src/VisualRelay.App/Theme/Colors.axaml` (new) — semantic color + brush tokens (accessibility-adjusted values)
- `src/VisualRelay.App/Theme/Typography.axaml` (new) — font-size tokens (12px floor)
- `src/VisualRelay.App/App.axaml` — add `<Application.Resources>` merging the token dictionaries
- `src/VisualRelay.App/Styles/VisualRelayTheme.axaml` — setters reference tokens
- `src/VisualRelay.App/Views/**/*.axaml` — inline color/`FontSize` literals → tokens (15 views carry
  `FontSize`; most carry hex)
- `src/VisualRelay.App/Views/Controls/ChevronIcon.cs`, `FocusToggleIcon.cs`,
  `Buttons/CommonButton.cs`, `ViewModels/TaskRowViewModel.cs`, `App.axaml.cs` — programmatic sites
- `tests/VisualRelay.Tests/DesignTokenCentralizationTests.cs` (new) — raw-literal guard + pure-classifier unit tests
- `tests/VisualRelay.Tests/DesignTokenAccessibilityTests.cs` (new) — WCAG contrast + min-size assertions
- `tests/VisualRelay.Tests/ChevronAffordanceRenderTests.cs` — update the foreground-default assertion


## Manifest

## Prior stages
## Stage 1 - Ideate

{
  "summary": "The task centralizes ~235 raw hex colors and ~107 inline FontSize values across an Avalonia UI app into named design tokens (Theme/Colors.axaml, Theme/Typography.axaml), adds a guard test that fails on raw literals outside those two files, and then adjusts the centralized values to meet WCAG 2.2 AA contrast and a 12px font-size floor — all as a single no-baseline, no-grandfathering commit. Three approaches differ in ordering (guard-first vs. inventory-first vs. tool-assisted transformation) and the up-front investment in automation.",
  "options": [
    "Option A (Strict sequential): Write the guard test first against pre-migration code → its failure output is the exact worklist → migrate every file on that list → guard goes green → then adjust accessibility values. Most conservative; matches spec order exactly; risk of guard-edge-cases delaying the worklist.",
    "Option B (Inventory-first): Grep-inventory all literals first, create tokens and migrate from that manifest, then write the guard as a regression barrier. Inverts the spec's order but gives a complete picture before any code changes; guard catches nothing new because migration is already done.",
    "Option C (Semi-automated rewrite): Extract a unique-value mapping, then script find-and-replace for each token across non-token AXAML files, handle C# and edge cases manually, write guard last. Reduces manual editing labor for 235+ replacements but risks over-matching and requires heavier up-front tooling."
  ]
}

## Stage 2 - Research

{
  "findings": "The codebase is an Avalonia 12.0.5 Dark-theme-only desktop app (.NET 10). **App.axaml** currently declares only `<Application.Styles>` (FluentTheme + VisualRelayTheme.axaml) — there is **no** `<Application.Resources>` section. No `Theme/` directory exists; `src/VisualRelay.App/Theme/` must be created. **VisualRelayTheme.axaml** (193 lines) carries ~48 raw hex color literals in `<Setter Property=... Value=\"#...\"/>` across 22 style selectors (Button, ListBoxItem, Border, TextBlock variants). It also has 2 `FontSize` literals (Value=\"12\" on panelTitle, Value=\"10\" on railTitle) and 3 `BoxShadow` hex colors. Overall, **~249 hex literal matches** appear across all `.axaml` files under `src/VisualRelay.App/` and **~119 `FontSize=\"...\"`** attribute matches. The distinct palette covers roughly 60+ unique hex strings (many repeats). Distinct font sizes in use: **10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 16**. C# programmatic sites: **ChevronIcon.cs** (line 48 `new SolidColorBrush(Color.Parse(\"#6F7785\"))` default + line 87 `Brushes.Gray` fallback), **FocusToggleIcon.cs** (line 77 `Brushes.Gray` fallback), **CommonButton.cs** (line 165 `FontSize = 14`), **App.axaml.cs** (line 109 `Brush.Parse(\"#1A1E26\")`, line 121 `FontSize = 13`, line 122 `Brush.Parse(\"#DCE2EA\")`), **TaskRowViewModel.cs** (lines 9-20 `Brush.Parse(...)` calls + line 74 `Brushes.Transparent`), **StageRowViewModel.cs** (lines 10-21 `Brush.Parse(...)` calls), **MainWindowViewModel.cs** (lines 15-22 `Brush.Parse(...)` calls). Additionally, **8 `GradientStop` hex colors** live in MainWindow.axaml (GridSplitter seam/grip styles) and **6 `BoxShadows.Parse` calls** in ViewModels with embedded hex colors. The repo has existing guard/test patterns: **ButtonsCentralizationTests.cs** discovers files via `RepoSetup.Root`, scans `.axaml` and `.cs` with regex, emits a numbered violation list, asserts zero. **FileSizeGuard.cs** (in `tools/VisualRelay.Guards/`) provides a pure `FindViolations((path, lines), limit)` function + real-tree `Enumerate` split, unit-tested by **FileSizeGuardTests.cs**. **ChevronAffordanceRenderTests.cs** has `ChevronForeground_HasExplicitDefault_NotNull` (line 192-197) which asserts `new ChevronIcon().Foreground` is non-null — this default is set in ChevronIcon.cs as `Color.Parse(\"#6F7785\")` and will need updating when that default moves to a Style. The `VisualRelay.Guards.csproj` is already a test dependency. The `VisualRelay.App.csproj` uses `<AvaloniaResource Include=\"Assets\\**\" />`; theme `ResourceDictionary` files in the same project are auto-included as Avalonia resources by convention without explicit declarations. The repository also has `FileSizeGuard` enforcing a 300-line limit per file.",

Includes error-panel colors `#2A1715`, `#F36F63`, `#F4B5AE`, `#17233D`, `#3191FF`, `#C7D4FF`.\n\n- **SettingsPanel.axaml** (295 lines): ~40 hex colors (contains 5 repeated provider-key rows each with green/grey dots `#5AD47D`/`#4A5568`, key labels `#E7ECF3`, muted `#7F8794`, hyperlink `#5575F2`). ~20 `FontSize` literals (11, 12, 13, 16).\n\n- **MainWindow.axaml** (199 lines): ~12 hex colors. Includes **8 GradientStop hex values** (lines 120-123 for idle seam: `#00252A33`, `#252A33`; lines 157-160 for hover seam: `#003191FF`, `#3191FF`) plus grip dots `#46535F` and `#5B9CFF`. 1 `FontSize=\"12\"` (banner).\n\n- **TopBar.axaml** (137 lines): ~12 hex colors. 8 `FontSize` literals, including `FontSize=\"10\"` on version text (line 39). Also has a `BoxShadow` with hex: `BoxShadow=\"0 10 24 0 #665575F2\"` (line 22).\n\n- **SandboxPaths.axaml** (133 lines): ~12 hex. ~12 `FontSize` literals across writable/readable/blocked path sections.\n\n- **StageBoard.axaml** (96 lines): ~6 hex. 5 `FontSize` literals (11).\n\n- **StageSystemView.axaml** (56 lines): ~4 hex. 3 `FontSize=\"13\"`.\n\n- **StageOutputView.axaml** (156 lines): ~6 hex. ~8 `FontSize` (11, 12, 13).\n\n- **StageInputView.axaml** (144 lines): ~6 hex. ~8 `FontSize` (11, 12, 13).\n\n- **RunLogView.axaml** (32 lines): ~3 hex (`#53B7F4`, `#7F8794`). 1 `FontSize=\"12\"`.\n\n- **CommandsView.axaml** (78 lines): ~7 hex. 3 `FontSize` (11, 12).\n\n- **ActivityColumn.axaml** (69 lines): ~3 hex. 2 `FontSize=\"11\"`.\n\n- **TaskActionBar.axaml** (43 lines): ~2 hex (`#8FE3A2`, `#8FA6FF`). 2 `FontSize=\"11\"`.\n\n- **RewriteToolbar.axaml** (40 lines): ~2 hex. 1 `FontSize=\"11\"`.\n\n- **ObsidianSettings.axaml** (38 lines): ~3 hex (`#E7ECF3`, `#9AA3B1`). 4 `FontSize` (11, 12).\n\n- **SettingsWindow.axaml** (32 lines): ~3 hex (`#1A1E26`, `#12151C`, `#252A33`).\n\n### 4. C# programmatic sites\n- **ChevronIcon.cs** line 48: `new SolidColorBrush(Color.Parse(\"#6F7785\"))` (StyledProperty default). Line 87: `Brushes.Gray` fallback.\n- **FocusToggleIcon.cs** line 77: `Brushes.Gray` fallback.\n- **CommonButton.cs** line 165: `FontSize = 14` (glyph TextBlock).\n- **App.axaml.cs** line 109: `Brush.Parse(\"#1A1E26\")`, line 121: `FontSize = 13`, line 122: `Brush.Parse(\"#DCE2EA\")`.\n- **TaskRowViewModel.cs** lines 9-17: 9 `Brush.Parse(...)` calls (`#3191FF`, `#5AD47D`, `#F2C66D`, `#171A20`, `#16233D`, `#14231B`, `#2A303A`). Line 18-20: 3 `BoxShadows.Parse(...)` with hex. Line 74: `Brushes.Transparent`.\n- **StageRowViewModel.cs** lines 10-18: 8 `Brush.Parse(...)` calls (`#7F8794`, `#5AD47D`, `#F36F63`, `#171A20`, `#14231B`, `#162B57`, `#2A303A`, `#3191FF`). Lines 19-21: 3 `BoxShadows.Parse(...)` with hex.\n- **MainWindowViewModel.cs** lines 15-22: 8 `Brush.Parse(...)` calls (pause button colors, backend status colors).\n\n### 5. Distinct palette\nFrom the grep output, ~70+ distinct hex values appear. Key semantic groups visible:\n- Backgrounds: `#0C0E12` (window), `#12151B`/`#12151C` (panels), `#1A1E25`/`#1A1E26` (buttons/dialogs), `#171A20`/`#171A20` (cards), `#15181F` (path button)\n- Border/separator: `#252A33` (very common), `#2A303A` (card borders), `#2B313C`, `#2A3040`, `#2A3340`\n- Text primary: `#DDE3EC`/`#DCE2EA`, `#E7ECF3`, `#F2F5FA`, `#FFFFFF`\n- Text muted: `#6F7785`, `#7F8794`, `#9AA3B1`, `#8E96A3`, `#5A6370`, `#6B7480`, `#C8CED8`\n- Accent: `#5B7CFA`/`#5575F2` (blue), `#3191FF` (selection), `#5AD47D` (green/success), `#F36F63`/`#E0556A` (red/error), `#F0CA66`/`#F2C66D` (yellow/warning)\n\n### 6. Existing test infrastructure\n- **ButtonsCentralizationTests.cs**: Model for the new guard. Uses `RepoSetup.Root`, enumerates `.axaml`/`.cs` files, applies regex scanning, emits numbered `relativePath:line → text` violation lists, asserts zero. Allow-lists a central home directory.\n- **FileSizeGuard.cs**: Pure `FindViolations(files, limit)` + real-tree `Enumerate(repoRoot, roots, limit)`. Unit-tested by `FileSizeGuardTests.cs` with in-memory TempDir.\n- **RepoSetup.cs**: Static `Root` property walks up from `AppContext.BaseDirectory` looking for `visual-relay` script file.\n- **VisualRelayThemeTests.cs**: Asserts `Contains(@\"Selector=\"\"Button.hyperlink\"\"\", content)` — must still pass after migration.\n- **ChevronAffordanceRenderTests.cs** lines 192-197: `ChevronForeground_HasExplicitDefault_NotNull` asserts `new ChevronIcon().Foreground` is non-null. The default `#6F7785` is set as a `StyledProperty` default at type-init time — cannot reference a `DynamicResource`. Must be relocated to a theme Style setter, and the test updated to check the tokenized value rather than deleted.\n\n### 7. FontSize distribution\n- `10`: Appears in **TopBar.axaml** line 39 (`FontSize=\"10\"` on version TextBlock) and **VisualRelayTheme.axaml** line 129 (`TextBlock.railTitle` setter).\n- `11`: Most common size. ~70+ occurrences across all views (QueuePanel, TaskDetailPanel, SettingsPanel, TopBar, StageBoard, SandboxPaths, ObsidianSettings, etc.).\n- `12`: ~20 occurrences (panelTitle style, various TextBlock/SelectableTextBlock).\n- `13`: ~20 occurrences (title TextBlocks, monospace content areas).\n- `14`: Only in **TopBar.axaml** line 32 (app title) and **CommonButton.cs** line 165 (glyph).\n- `16`: In **SettingsPanel.axaml** lines 21 and 70 (section headers).\n\n### 8. Accessibility concerns (pre-migration)\n- `FontSize=\"10\"` and `FontSize=\"11\"` are below the 12px floor — must be raised.\n- Contrast offenders likely: muted text `#6F7785` (rgb 111,119,133) on dark backgrounds, warning `#F0CA66` on `#2B2416`, accent `#5B7CFA`/`#5575F2` — need WCAG verification.\n- The 12px floor will affect rail title (currently 10px) and the most common body size (currently 11px) — deliberate visible change.",

  "repro": "## Reproduction / verification steps\n\n### Verify current state (pre-migration)\n1. Confirm no Theme directory: `ls src/VisualRelay.App/Theme/` → `No such file or directory`\n2. Confirm no Application.Resources in App.axaml: `grep -c 'Application.Resources' src/VisualRelay.App/App.axaml` → `0`\n3. Count raw hex in theme: `grep -c '#[0-9A-Fa-f]\\{6,8\\}' src/VisualRelay.App/Styles/VisualRelayTheme.axaml` → ~48\n4. Count raw hex across all views: `grep -r '#[0-9A-Fa-f]\\{6,8\\}' src/VisualRelay.App/Views/ --include='*.axaml' | wc -l` → ~200\n5. Count FontSize literals: `grep -r 'FontSize=\"[0-9]' src/VisualRelay.App/Views/ --include='*.axaml' | wc -l` → ~107\n6. Count Brush.Parse in C#: `grep -r 'Brush\\.Parse' src/VisualRelay.App/ --include='*.cs' | wc -l` → 27\n7. Run existing tests to confirm baseline green: `dotnet test tests/VisualRelay.Tests/`\n\n### What the migration must accomplish\n1. Create `src/VisualRelay.App/Theme/Colors.axaml` with semantic Color+SolidColorBrush resources\n2. Create `src/VisualRelay.App/Theme/Typography.axaml` with x:Double font-size resources\n3. Add `<Application.Resources>` to App.axaml merging both via ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries\n4. Replace all hex/FontSize literals in VisualRelayTheme.axaml with DynamicResource/StaticResource references\n5. Replace all inline hex/FontSize in 19 view .axaml files with resource references\n6. Resolve programmatic C# sites: move ChevronIcon default to Style setter, resolve tokens from Application.Current.FindResource for code-built UI\n7. Add DesignTokenCentralizationTests.cs guard (pure classifier + real-tree scan)\n8. Add DesignTokenAccessibilityTests.cs (WCAG contrast function + pairing assertions + 12px floor)\n9. Tidy token values: raise 10 and 11 to 12, fix contrast ratios to WCAG 2.2 AA\n10. Update ChevronForeground_HasExplicitDefault_NotNull test assertion\n11. Run full test suite → all green, guard reports 0 violations"
}

## Stage 4 - Plan

{
  "plan": "## Implementation plan (13 steps)\n\n### Step 1 — Add the guard (red on pre-migration tree)\nCreate `tools/VisualRelay.Guards/DesignTokenGuard.cs` with pure static methods:\n- `FindHexViolations(files)`: scans `.axaml` lines for hex (`#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6,8}`) outside the two allow-listed token files (`Theme/Colors.axaml`, `Theme/Typography.axaml`). Returns `(path, line, text)` for each offender.\n- `FindFontSizeLiteralViolations(files)`: scans `.axaml` for `FontSize=\"<number>\"` or `<Setter Property=\"FontSize\" Value=\"<number>\"` (value not a `{StaticResource …}` or `{DynamicResource …}`). Returns violations.\n- `FindNamedColorViolations(files)`: scans brush-bearing attributes (`Foreground`, `Background`, `BorderBrush`, `Fill`, `Stroke`) for named-color keywords (`Red`, `White`, `Gray`, …) that aren't `Transparent` or `{x:Null}` and aren't resource references. Returns violations.\n- `FindCsViolations(files)`: scans `src/VisualRelay.App/**/*.cs` for `Color.Parse(\"`, `Color.FromRgb(`, `Color.FromArgb(`, `Colors.<Name>`, `Brushes.<Name>`, `new SolidColorBrush(`, `Brush.Parse(\"`, `BoxShadows.Parse(\"`, `FontSize = <number>`. Allow-lists no extra files (all C# resolves from `Application.Current!.FindResource` or moves to Style setters).\n\nCreate `tests/VisualRelay.Tests/DesignTokenCentralizationTests.cs` modeled on `ButtonsCentralizationTests.cs`:\n- **In-memory unit tests** (pure classifier): a hex line fails, `{DynamicResource SurfaceBrush}` passes, `Transparent` passes, `{x:Null}` passes, `FontSize=\"12\"` fails, `FontSize=\"{StaticResource FontSizeBody}\"` passes, `Width=\"12\"` passes, `Red` on Foreground fails, `Color.Parse(\"#ABC\")` in C# fails.\n- **Real-tree `[Fact]`**: `DesignTokenGuard_NoRawLiterals_InAppSource()` — discovers repo via `RepoSetup.Root`, enumerates all `.axaml`/`.cs` under `src/VisualRelay.App`, runs the pure classifiers, asserts zero violations with a numbered worklist message. Allow-lists `Theme/Colors.axaml` and `Theme/Typography.axaml`.\n\nThis test will be **red** (~200+ violations) — its output is the migration worklist.\n\n### Step 2 — Create `Theme/Colors.axaml` (value-preserving)\nCreate directory `src/VisualRelay.App/Theme/`. Create `Colors.axaml` as a `ResourceDictionary` defining a semantic `Color` + matching `SolidColorBrush` pair for every distinct hex value in the codebase. Collapse exact duplicates (`#2A303A` → `CardBorderBrush`). Group by role: Surfaces (WindowBgBrush #0C0E12, PanelBgBrush #12151B, SurfaceBrush #1A1E25, CardBgBrush #171A20, …), Borders (BorderSubtleBrush #252A33, CardBorderBrush #2A303A, ButtonBorderBrush #2B313C, …), Text (TextPrimaryBrush #DDE3EC, TextHeadingBrush #F2F5FA, TextSubtleBrush #6F7785, TextMutedBrush #9AA3B1, …), Accent (AccentBrush #5B7CFA, SelectionBrush #3191FF, FocusToggleTextBrush #C7D4FF, …), Semantic (SuccessBrush #5AD47D, ErrorBrush #F36F63, WarningBrush #F0CA66, …). Also define `BoxShadows` resources: `PanelShadow`, `NoShadow`, `SelectedGlow22`, `SelectedGlow20`, `RunningGlow22`, `RunningGlow20`, `LogoGlow`. Keep ≤300 lines with `<!-- -->` section comments.\n\n### Step 3 — Create `Theme/Typography.axaml` (value-preserving)\n`ResourceDictionary` with `x:Double` tokens: `FontSizeCaption=10`, `FontSizeBody=11`, `FontSizeBodyLarge=12`, `FontSizeSubtitle=13`, `FontSizeTitle=14`, `FontSizeHeading=16`. One token per distinct size, named by role.\n\n### Step 4 — Wire `App.axaml`\nAdd `<Application.Resources><ResourceDictionary><ResourceDictionary.MergedDictionaries>` with `<ResourceInclude Source=\"avares://VisualRelay.App/Theme/Colors.axaml\"/>` and Typography, placed before `<Application.Styles>`.\n\n### Step 5 — Migrate `VisualRelayTheme.axaml`\nReplace every raw hex `Value=\"#…\"` with `Value=\"{DynamicResource …Brush}\"`. Add two new Style selectors: `controls|ChevronIcon` and `controls|FocusToggleIcon` with Foreground setters using tokens (relocated from C# defaults). Replace `FontSize` literals with `{StaticResource …}`. Replace `BoxShadow` literals with `{DynamicResource …}`.\n\n### Step 6 — Migrate 17 view `.axaml` files\nFor each view file, replace `Foreground=\"#…\"` / `Background=\"#…\"` / `BorderBrush=\"#…\"` / `Fill=\"#…\"` with `{DynamicResource …Brush}`, `FontSize=\"N\"` with `{StaticResource …}`, `BoxShadow=\"…\"` with `{DynamicResource …}`. GradientStop `Color=\"#00252A33\"` and `Color=\"#003191FF\"` (alpha=0) → `Transparent`; others → `{StaticResource …Color}`. TopBar.axaml line 22 BoxShadow → token.\n\n### Step 7 — Migrate C# sites\n- **ChevronIcon.cs**: Remove `defaultValue: new SolidColorBrush(Color.Parse(\"#6F7785\"))` from `ForegroundProperty` registration. Change line 87 `Foreground ?? Brushes.Gray` → `Foreground ?? Application.Current!.FindResource(\"TextSubtleBrush\") as IBrush`.\n- **FocusToggleIcon.cs**: Change line 77 `Foreground ?? Brushes.Gray` → `Foreground ?? Application.Current!.FindResource(\"TextSubtleBrush\") as IBrush`.\n- **CommonButton.cs**: Line 165 `FontSize = 14` → `FontSize = (double)Application.Current!.FindResource(\"FontSizeTitle\")`.\n- **App.axaml.cs**: Lines 109,121,122 — resolve from `Application.Current!.FindResource(…)`.\n- **TaskRowViewModel.cs**: Convert 9 `Brush.Parse(…)` + 3 `BoxShadows.Parse(…)` + `Brushes.Transparent` → lazy `Lazy<IBrush>`/`Lazy<BoxShadows>` with `FindResource`. `Brushes.Transparent` stays (exempt).\n- **StageRowViewModel.cs**: Same pattern — 8 `Brush.Parse` + 3 `BoxShadows.Parse` → `Lazy<T>` with `FindResource`.\n- **MainWindowViewModel.cs**: 8 `Brush.Parse` → `Lazy<IBrush>` with `FindResource`.\n\n### Step 8 — Update `ChevronAffordanceRenderTests.cs`\nUpdate `ChevronForeground_HasExplicitDefault_NotNull` (line 192-197): since the C# default moved to a theme Style setter, the test now verifies the resolved styled value matches the token color `#6F7785` (TextSubtleBrush). Use the headless session with app resources loaded.\n\n### Step 9 — Verify guard is green\n`dotnet test --filter DesignTokenCentralizationTests` → 0 violations. Full suite: `dotnet test` → all existing tests green. `VisualRelayThemeTests.Theme_ContainsHyperlinkButtonStyle` still finds `Button.hyperlink` selector.\n\n### Step 10 — Accessibility: contrast fix\nAudit fg/bg token pairs in `Colors.axaml` against WCAG 2.2 AA (4.5:1 normal, 3:1 large/UI). Adjust token values where needed (e.g., lighten TextSubtleBrush from #6F7785 to meet 4.5:1 on PanelBgBrush). Only token values change; all consumers update automatically.\n\n### Step 11 — Accessibility: raise type floor\nIn `Typography.axaml`, change `FontSizeCaption` 10→12, `FontSizeBody` 11→12. No font-size token remains below 12.\n\n### Step 12 — Create `DesignTokenAccessibilityTests.cs`\nPure WCAG functions (`RelativeLuminance`, `ContrastRatio`) with unit tests (black-on-white=21:1). Real-tree `[Fact]` parses `Colors.axaml` and `Typography.axaml`, checks explicit fg/bg pairings against AA thresholds, and asserts no font-size token < 12.\n\n### Step 13 — Final verification\n`dotnet test` — all gates green: centralization guard 0 violations, accessibility guard all pairs pass, FileSizeGuard no file >300 lines, all existing tests pass.",

result.answer:
The test results are exactly as expected for the author-tests stage:

- **36 passed** — all pure unit tests pass (classifier logic is correct)
- **2 failed** — both real-tree scans fail correctly:
  1. `DesignTokenGuard_NoRawLiterals_InAppSource` enumerates 409+ violations (the migration worklist)
  2. `DesignTokens_MeetAccessibilityThresholds` fails because token files don't exist yet

This is the correct "red on pre-migration tree" state. The guard first run produces the comprehensive worklist; the pure classifiers are proven correct by in-memory tests.

```json
{
  "testFiles": [
    "tools/VisualRelay.Guards/DesignTokenGuard.cs",
    "tests/VisualRelay.Tests/DesignTokenCentralizationTests.cs",
    "tests/VisualRelay.Tests/DesignTokenCentralizationGuardTests.cs",
    "tests/VisualRelay.Tests/DesignTokenAccessibilityTests.cs",
    "tests/VisualRelay.[ENCRYPTED:aws-secret-key]GSfl+N6x9K2NXBlnuLKi4pMiv1movLffibqWu8qB.cs"
  ],
  "rationale": "Created the DesignTokenGuard with pure classification functions (FindHexViolations, FindFontSizeLiteralViolations, FindNamedColorViolations, FindCsViolations) following the FileSizeGuard pattern. The guard enforces four rules: (1) no #-hex in .axaml outside the two token files, (2) FontSize must be a resource reference, (3) no named-color keywords on brush-bearing attributes, (4) no programmatic color/size literals in C#. DesignTokenCentralizationTests provides in-memory unit tests for each rule. DesignTokenCentralizationGuardTests runs the real-tree scan over src/VisualRelay.App — currently failing with 409+ violations, which is the migration worklist. DesignTokenAccessibilityTests provides pure WCAG 2.2 contrast math with unit tests (black-on-white = 21:1, identical = 1:1). DesignTokenAccessibilityGuardTests will parse token files and verify AA thresholds once tokens exist. All 5 files are under 300 lines. 36/38 tests pass; the 2 failures are the expected pre-migration guard failures."
}
```
